data.title: Malaysia Gold Prices Slide Despite Global Rally
data.description:
Gold prices in Malaysia eased slightly, with the price per kilogram falling 3.03% to RM14,279.84, or RM646.87 per gram, as of early New York trading hours.

The decline marks a small retracement from recent gains amid heightened safe-haven demand globally.

Silver prices in Malaysia also softened, down 3% to RM646.87 per kilogram, after surging in previous sessions to near record highs.

Globally, gold hit an all-time high of US$5,110.50 per ounce on Monday, driven by geopolitical uncertainty and safe-haven demand.

Silver surged 8% to US$117.69 per ounce, marking a rise of more than 50% so far this year.

Analysts attributed the rally to escalating trade tensions, including US threats of higher tariffs on Canada and South Korea, and concerns over a possible US government shutdown.

RHB Investment Bank said COMEX gold showed strong bullish momentum after breaching the US$5,000 resistance for the first time, with analysts maintaining a positive outlook.

“After the breakout, the bulls will eye to test the US$5,200 mark, followed by the US$5,500 threshold,” RHB noted.

In Malaysia, gold has seen strong gains over the past year, rising 66.3% over the last 12 months and up 171% over five years, reflecting the metal’s role as a hedge against global uncertainty and currency fluctuations.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum Cautious Ahead Of Macro Signals
data.description:
Bitcoin and Ethereum showed limited gains and consolidation on Jan 28 as the broader cryptocurrency market digested macroeconomic uncertainty and awaited key catalysts.

Bitcoin ticked slightly higher within a narrow range near the upper US$80,000s, reflecting subdued buyer conviction and continued risk-off positioning among traders and institutional players. Ethereum outperformed modestly, posting small gains while trading around the mid-US$2,900s amid selective altcoin interest. Overall, the market cap held near the US$3 trillion mark, signalling stabilisation after recent volatility.

Market Dynamics Driving the Pause

Macro risk sensitivity: Investors are positioning cautiously ahead of major Federal Reserve (Fed) guidance and other economic data, tempering crypto risk exposure and favouring traditional assets. Safe-haven flows into gold and other low-risk instruments have influenced digital asset sentiment.

Cost basis and technical support: On-chain data indicates Ethereum trading within a significant cost-basis cluster, potentially reinforcing short-term support while highlighting thinner layers of holder conviction beneath key levels.

Sentiment rebound signs: Broader market analysis suggests the crypto downturn that marked late 2025 may have bottomed, with some strategic positioning indicating renewed, albeit cautious, interest from institutional allocators.

What Investors Are Watching

Fed outcomes: Interest rate expectations and liquidity guidance from the Fed’s latest meeting remain pivotal for near-term price direction.

Volatility trends: Recent week’s stabilisation points to reduced, not eliminated, volatility with range-bound trading likely until clearer macro cues emerge.

Institutional activity: Fund flows and strategic positioning by large holders and institutional funds will continue shaping market breadth.

Bottom Line: Bitcoin and Ethereum exhibited measured price action amid a cautious market backdrop. With macroeconomic signals and liquidity expectations at the forefront of investor focus, the digital asset sector remains in a strategic consolidation phase, with participants awaiting clarity before committing to new directional positions.

data.title: SGX Opens Lower On Profit-Taking, STI Down 21 Points
data.description:
The Singapore Exchange (SGX) opened lower on Tuesday, with the Straits Times Index (STI) slipping 21.55 points, or 0.44%, to 4,901.47 at 9.05 am.

Market breadth was positive, with 106 advancers outpacing 82 decliners, while trading volume reached 121.22 million shares valued at S$214.05 million.

Among the notable early movers, DBS rose 0.06 to S$59.33, while Singtel traded at S$4.56 and ST Engineering at S$9.57. OCBC Bank and UOB were at S$21.36 and S$38.41 respectively, with YZJ Shipbuilding at S$3.34.

On the derivatives front, MSCI Singapore Index Futures for February 2026 were last seen at 462.55, while FTSE China A50 Index Futures for January 2026 traded at 14,851.00. Nikkei 225 Index Futures were at 52,975.00, and FTSE Blossom Japan Index Futures at 293.45.

FX futures showed USD/SGD at 1.2647, USD/CNH at 6.9203, and KRW/USD at 0.6987, reflecting a steady start for the local dollar against major currencies.

Investors are watching for global cues, including U.S. economic data and regional earnings updates, which could influence the Singapore market’s direction in the session ahead.

data.title: Bursa Opens Higher, FBM KLCI Gains 8.77 Points
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened higher, led by early buying in blue-chip counters as investors appeared cautious but optimistic ahead of the corporate earnings season.

The benchmark FBM KLCI (FBMKLCI) climbed 8.77 points, or 0.50%, to 1,752.84, while the FBM70 added 25.92 points, or 0.15%, to 17,656.75. The F4GBM Index rose 4.29 points, or 0.42%, to 1,035.20, and the FBMEMAS index gained 51.37 points, or 0.40%, to 12,850.52. The FBMSHA edged up 20 points, or 0.16%, to 12,420.39.

Among the most active stocks, Greentec (7943) led trading with 8.34 million shares, gaining 1.5 sen to 25 sen. Capital A (5099) followed with 8.10 million shares at 59.5 sen, up half a sen.

One Gasmaster Holdings (0389), which made its ACE Market debut earlier, traded 4.54 million shares at 23 sen, down 2 sen from its opening.

Top gainers in early trade included CIMB (1023), up 13 sen to RM8.75, LPI Capital (8621) rising 12 sen to RM15.36, and AAX (5238), which added 11 sen to RM2.30.

On the flip side, UTDPLT (2089) slipped 14 sen to RM30.72, MPI (3867) fell 8 sen to RM32.50, and QL Resources (7084) lost 7 sen to RM4.18.

The early market sentiment suggests measured optimism, with investors weighing corporate earnings announcements against macroeconomic developments.

data.title: SGX Opens Higher, STI Gains 0.63% In Early Trade
data.description:
The Singapore Exchange (SGX) started the week on a positive note, with the Straits Times Index (STI) climbing 30.41 points, or 0.63%, to 4,891.34 in early trade.

Market activity showed 99 advancing stocks against 49 decliners, with a total trading volume of 68.15 million shares valued at S$147.22 million.

Leading the gains were blue chips DBS, which rose 0.76 points to S$59.05 (+1.3%), and UOB, trading at S$38.43. Singtel added ground at S$4.54, while OCBC Bank was steady at S$21.19. Shipbuilding stocks YZJ Shipbuilding and Nam Cheong also saw early buying interest.

In derivatives, equity index futures reflected steady activity, with the MSCI Singapore Index Futures at 459.55 and Nikkei 225 Futures at 52,915.00, while FX and commodity futures tracked moderate early movements.

The market opened firm amid cautious optimism from investors, following strong regional cues and a broadly positive start in Asia Pacific equities.

data.title: Bursa Eyes Higher Ground As KLCI Gains 16 Points At Midday
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended its gains by midday, with the benchmark FBM KLCI rising 15.85 points or 0.91% to 1,759.92, led by positive sentiment across both large-cap and mid-cap stocks.

The broader market followed suit, with the FBM70 advancing 85.98 points or 0.49% to 17,716.81, while the FBMEMAS gained 98.53 points or 0.77% to 12,897.68.

Activity was concentrated in the construction, banking, and industrial sectors.

Among the most actively traded counters, CAPITALA slipped 0.015 to 0.575 with 53.8 million shares changing hands, while SP Setia added 0.020 to 0.975 on 37.2 million shares.

Debutant OGM saw a notable decline of 0.040 to 0.210 with 32.3 million shares traded, reflecting some profit-taking.

HLFG emerged as the top gainer, climbing 0.520 to RM21.82 on moderate volume of 253,000 shares. MPI also added 0.520 to RM33.10, while MCEMENT rose 0.280 to RM8.03 on 1.33 million shares.

Conversely, Nestle dipped 0.600 to RM115.40, leading the losers, followed by Carlsberg down 0.260 to RM18.08 and UTDPLT losing 0.260 to RM30.60.

Market watchers noted that broad-based buying reflected cautious optimism among investors amid steady regional cues and recovering corporate earnings.

The FBMSHA index of heavyweights advanced 57.65 points or 0.46% to 12,458.04, signalling support from large-cap stocks.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Rallies On FBM KLCI, FBM EMAS Surges Over 1%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia closed higher today, buoyed by broad-based gains as investor sentiment remained positive. The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBMKLCI) rose 27.18 points, or 1.56%, to finish at 1,771.25.

The broader market also advanced, with the FBM EMAS adding 155.55 points (+1.22%) to 12,954.70 and the F4GBM index gaining 13.27 points (+1.29%) to 1,044.18.

Mid- and small-cap stocks saw more modest gains, with the FBM70 up 83.24 points (+0.47%) to 17,714.07 and FBMSHA rising 87.82 points (+0.71%) to 12,488.21.

Among the most active counters, Borneo Oil (BORNOIL) led trading activity with 191.98 million shares changing hands, followed by Capital A (CAPITALA) at 68.62 million shares.

Tanco Holdings (TANCO) gained 0.02 to RM1.33, while Public Bank (PBBANK) rose 0.13 to RM5.00. SP Setia (SPSETIA) added 0.025 to close at RM0.98.

The market’s positive close was supported by buying interest across key sectors, reflecting cautious optimism from both domestic and foreign investors.

data.title: Global Stocks Touch Fresh Records As Dollar Slides Ahead Of Fed
data.description:
Global equity markets extended their rally on Tuesday, with stocks climbing for a fifth straight session and touching record levels, as investors turned increasingly optimistic over US corporate earnings.

At the same time, the US dollar weakened sharply, sinking to its lowest point in nearly four years ahead of the Federal Reserve’s policy announcement.

On Wall Street, trading was mixed but largely positive. The S&P 500 closed at a record high, while the Nasdaq Composite also advanced, supported by strong earnings sentiment. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, however, ended lower after being dragged down by sharp declines in select heavyweights.

The Dow fell 408.99 points or 0.83% to 49,003.41. In contrast, the S&P 500 rose 28.37 points or 0.41% to 6,978.60, while the Nasdaq gained 215.74 points or 0.91% to 23,817.10.

Losses in Boeing and UnitedHealth weighed heavily on the Dow. Boeing slid 1.6%, while UnitedHealth plunged about 20% after warning that its 2026 revenue would shrink. The decline in UnitedHealth alone accounted for roughly 420 points of downward pressure on the index. Health insurers broadly came under pressure following a lower-than-expected Medicare reimbursement proposal for 2027.

Meanwhile, investors continued to assess a wave of major earnings releases, including results from Boeing and United Parcel Service, which said it would cut up to 30,000 operational roles this year. Attention is now turning to upcoming earnings from the so-called Magnificent Seven, including Microsoft, Apple, Tesla and Meta Platforms.

Beyond the US, global markets also pushed higher. MSCI’s gauge of stocks worldwide rose 0.85% to 1,053.09, marking its longest winning streak this year. The index hit an intraday record of 1,053.88. In Europe, the STOXX 600 ended up 0.58%, supported by a 1.8% jump in banking stocks.

Currency markets were dominated by a sharp slide in the greenback. The dollar index fell 0.9% to 96.23, after touching a four-year low of 95.566, putting it on track for its biggest daily percentage drop since April. The euro strengthened 0.81% to US$1.1975, while sterling climbed 1.14% to US$1.3833.

“The dollar has had over the past ten days one of the biggest moves it’s had in the past five years… It puts a huge pressure on commodities, and we’re seeing oil up. We’re seeing commodities up, and you’re seeing all those stocks moving up in a very aggressive way,” said Adam Rich, deputy chief investment officer and portfolio manager at Vaughan Nelson Investment Management in Houston.

The dollar also weakened against the Japanese yen, sliding 1.23% to 152.26, its lowest level since late October. The Korean won strengthened 0.97% to 1,431.85 per dollar after earlier losses, as South Korea sought to reassure the US over its commitment to a trade deal following fresh tariff threats from President Donald Trump.

Markets are now bracing for the Federal Reserve’s policy statement, due on Wednesday. While the central bank is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged, political developments surrounding the Fed leadership have added an extra layer of uncertainty.

Commodities rallied strongly amid the weaker dollar and ongoing geopolitical concerns. Spot gold surged 3.19% to US$5,174.09 an ounce after hitting a record intraday high, while spot silver jumped 7.93% to US$121.13 an ounce.

U.S. crude oil settled up 2.9% at US$62.39 a barrel, while Brent crude gained 3.02% to US$67.57 per barrel, as a winter storm disrupted production and halted US Gulf Coast crude exports over the weekend.

Source: Reuters

data.title: Chinese Yuan Rises Against USD, Trades At 6.97
data.description:
The central parity rate of the Chinese currency renminbi, or the yuan, strengthened 103 pips to 6.9755 against the U.S. dollar Wednesday, according to the China Foreign Exchange Trade System.

In China’s spot foreign exchange market, the yuan is allowed to rise or fall by 2 percent from the central parity rate each trading day.

The central parity rate of the yuan against the U.S. dollar is based on a weighted average of prices offered by market makers before the opening of the interbank market each business day.

data.title: Asian Stocks Track Wall St Gains, Seoul Brushes Off Tariff Threat
data.description:
Asian markets rose Tuesday following gains on Wall Street, with tech firms leading Seoul to another record as investors brushed off Donald Trump’s threat to hike tariffs on South Korean goods.

The yen held its gains after a two-day surge stoked by intervention talk, while geopolitical and economic uncertainty saw silver hit another fresh peak and gold hover just below its own high.

Traders are also gearing up for a Federal Reserve policy meeting and earnings from tech titans, which will be pored over for an idea about sustainability of the AI investment surge.

Equities enjoyed healthy buying despite the US president reverting to tariff threats, warning South Korea he would impose 25 percent tolls on goods including autos for falling short of expectations on an earlier pact struck with Washington.

The announcement comes months after the two sides struck a trade and security deal following tense negotiations, setting levies at 15 percent.

“South Korea’s Legislature is not living up to its Deal with the United States,” Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform.

He added that he was increasing tariff rates “because the Korean Legislature hasn’t enacted our Historic Trade Agreement, which is their prerogative.”

The presidential office in Seoul said it had not been informed in advance but added that Trade Minister Kim Jung-kwan, currently in Canada, would head to Washington for talks with US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.

Trump’s outburst follows a warning to Canada on Saturday that it faced 100 percent levies if it signed a trade deal with China, days after backing down from a threat to hit several European countries with measures over their opposition to his grab for Greenland.

Still, Seoul’s Kospi continued its run to fresh record highs, with observers pointing to the US president’s history of rowing back the worst of his threats.

While carmakers slipped, tech firms ploughed higher with chipmaking giant SK hynix up more than five percent and Samsung Electronics up two percent.

There were also big gains in Hong Kong, Shanghai, Sydney, Singapore, Taipei, Manila and Jakarta.

Tech firms are enjoying a fresh boost ahead of earnings releases as traders continue to pile into all things AI.

Magnificent Seven members Apple, Microsoft, Meta and Tesla are due this week, with other bellwethers including Texas Instruments, Boeing and Mastercard providing an idea about the state of the economy.

However, with questions being asked about the amount of cash being invested in artificial intelligence, there is a little nervousness on trading floors about when profits will be realised.

“The AI capex cycle is increasingly colliding with the real world: debt markets, power grids, and regulation,” wrote Matt Weller, head of market research at City Index.

He added that “2026 capex estimates for the largest ‘hyperscalers’ is widely forecast to hit the $600 bn+ range, driven primarily by AI infrastructure. At the same time, major tech firms have leaned more heavily into debt issuance to fund the infrastructure race”.

“This matters for earnings because the market’s attention is moving from ‘who spends the most’ to ‘who can sustain the spend without eroding free cash flow’, especially if AI monetisation takes longer than expected.”

Developments in Washington are also being followed after some senators warned they would vote against upcoming spending bills following the second killing of a US citizen in Minneapolis, threatening another possible government shutdown.

The dollar remained under pressure after its latest selloff sparked by talk of a joint intervention between US and Japanese authorities to support the yen.

And in corporate news, Hong Kong-listed shares in China’s Zijin Gold International rose more than one percent after it agreed to buy Allied Gold, which owns gold mines in Africa, for US$4 billion. Its parent, Zijin Mining Group, soared more than six percent.

Zijin Gold’s shares have tripled since listing in September.

data.title: MPI Leads Gains, Nestle Slides
data.description:
MPI emerged as the top gainer on Bursa Malaysia today, climbing 0.64 to RM33.22 with 225,300 shares traded, followed by Hong Leong Bank (HLBANK) which rose 0.58 to RM25.08 on higher activity of 2.42 million shares.

Other notable gainers included Hong Leong Financial Group (HLFG) up 0.56 to RM21.86, CIMB Group (CIMB) added 0.33 to RM8.95, and Malayan Cement (MCEMENT) increased 0.29 to RM8.04.

On the losing side, Nestle led declines, slipping 0.40 to RM115.60 with 73,800 shares changing hands. Carlsberg (CARLSBG) fell 0.26 to RM18.08, followed by KLCC Property (KLCC) down 0.20 to RM9.16, Kuala Lumpur Kepong (KLK) down 0.20 to RM19.82, and Bintulu Port Holdings (BIPORT) losing 0.15 to RM5.35

data.title: Dollar Faces Pressure As Trump Policies And Geopolitical Risks Shake Investor Confidence
data.description:
The US dollar is under renewed pressure in early 2026 as investors reassess the impact of President Donald Trump’s policies and escalating geopolitical tensions.

On Monday, the dollar was set for its largest three-day slide against a basket of major currencies since April 2025, reflecting growing market uncertainty.

Trump’s aggressive trade stance, challenges to Federal Reserve independence and significant increases in public spending have all contributed to the greenback’s volatility.

So far this year, the dollar has lagged behind major rivals such as the euro, sterling and Swiss franc.

“There are a number of factors coming together and faster than expected,” said Seema Shah, chief global strategist at Principal Asset Management.

Market attention has been drawn to Trump’s threats on Greenland, European tariffs and trade tensions with Canada, alongside domestic issues including immigration crackdowns and the risk of a government shutdown.

Investors are also factoring in anticipated Federal Reserve rate cuts, which make US assets less attractive relative to other markets.

Powell’s planned departure in May has further fueled speculation about his successor, with BlackRock’s Rick Rieder, a proponent of lower rates, now viewed as a frontrunner.

Meanwhile, global equities have outperformed US markets over the past year, with the S&P 500 rising around 15% since Trump’s inauguration compared with gains of 95% in Seoul’s Kospi, 40% in Tokyo’s Nikkei and nearly 30% in Shanghai’s main index.

“The trend is clear. Asset managers are keen to diversify away from the US,” said Chris Scicluna, economist at Daiwa Capital Markets.

Chris added, “Many had been overweight US markets and are now rebalancing.”

The dollar’s slide underscores mounting investor caution amid policy uncertainty and geopolitical risks, prompting a search for alternative safe havens such as gold and Japanese government bonds.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates Jan 27, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on Jan 27, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Tech Sector Unlikely Impacted By Stronger Ringgit
data.description:
The technology sector is expected to see moderate growth in 2026, with Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) maintaining a NEUTRAL call on the KLTEC index while highlighting ITMAX, UWC, and Frontken as top sector picks.

The research house noted, from a recent field trip to 12 Penang-based companies, that management teams are broadly optimistic for 2026, with on-the-ground activity indicating that China+1 diversification is more advanced than previously anticipated.

Positive feedback from global semiconductor players, including Intel, AMD, Lam Research, Infineon, SanDisk and Micron, points to rising engagement and capacity allocation in Malaysia, reinforcing its role as a strategic production and R&D hub.

The field trip also indicated that US semiconductor tariffs are no longer a major concern following the deferment of China-related Section 301 tariffs to June 2027.

Apple’s accelerated shift of iPhone production to India is creating tangible opportunities for Malaysian suppliers, with companies such as ViTrox already deriving around 5–7% of sales from India.

Expansion by other multinational corporations is expected to support further growth for Malaysian EMS, OSAT, and equipment suppliers, while a robust pipeline of tech-related IPOs, including SkyeChip and NSW Automation, could add capital and expansion momentum in 2026 and beyond.

Despite concerns over the strong ringgit, HLIB noted that historical data show only a weak negative correlation between the KLTEC index and RM/USD, with sector performance driven more by global semiconductor cycles and US Federal Reserve policies.

The upcoming 4Q25 earnings season is expected to reflect a reasonable bar for large-cap companies, with investor attention likely focused on management guidance for 2026 and margin expectations.

HLIB highlighted that stock selection remains critical, as stronger ringgit pressures could widen the gap between outperformers and laggards, making a selective approach essential in the current environment.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Firmer On Dollar Weakness
data.description:
The Malaysian ringgit opened stronger against the US dollar on Wednesday, boosted by weaker-than-expected US economic data that continued to pressure the greenback.

At 8 am, the local currency was quoted at 3.9485/9500 versus the US dollar, a level not seen since May 2018, compared with Tuesday’s close of 3.9500/9555.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US Dollar Index (DXY) slid 1.32% to 95.759 points, following data that reinforced expectations of monetary easing later this year.

“The US Conference Board’s Consumer Confidence Index fell more than expected to 84.5 points in January, below the consensus estimate of 90.6 points,” he said, adding that Americans have grown increasingly concerned about the rising cost of living and weaker job prospects. “This suggests consumer spending — a key pillar of the US economy — is likely to continue moderating.”

Dr Mohd Afzanizam also noted that the Japanese yen had strengthened amid ongoing speculation over possible coordinated intervention by the Japanese government and the US Federal Reserve. He said the ringgit could breach the 3.95 level against the US dollar if dollar weakness persists.

Against other major currencies and regional peers, the ringgit opened mixed. It eased against the British pound to 5.4592/4613 from 5.4103/4178 at Tuesday’s close, fell versus the euro to 4.7473/7491 from 4.6958/7023, and weakened against the Japanese yen to 2.5890/5902 from 2.5691/5730.

The local currency was lower against the Singapore dollar at 3.1317/1332 from 3.1174/1219, slipped versus the Thai baht to 12.7639/7790 from 12.7247/7478, edged up against the Indonesian rupiah to 235.4/235.6 from 235.5/236.0, and remained flat against the Philippine peso at 6.68/6.69.

Bernama

data.title: Wall Street Extends Gains Ahead Of Mega-Cap Earnings, Fed Meeting
data.description:
Wall Street edged higher on Monday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq logging their fourth consecutive session of gains as investors prepared for a wave of mega-cap earnings and a Federal Reserve policy update later this week.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 313.69 points, or 0.64%, to 49,412.40, while the S&P 500 gained 34.62 points, or 0.50%, to 6,950.23. The Nasdaq Composite added 100.11 points, or 0.43%, to 23,601.36. Both indexes hit their highest levels in over a week, marking their longest string of advances since December.

Big tech led the rally, with Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta and Broadcom providing the largest boosts to the S&P 500. Investors are closely watching these names for signs of measurable returns on AI spending, with upcoming quarterly results seen as a key test for the tech-driven rally.

“Communications and technology are trading well in advance of earnings from many large companies,” said Chris Zaccarelli, chief investment officer at Northlight Asset Management. “There’s cautious optimism ahead of earnings season.”

Meanwhile, individual sectors showed mixed performance. Communications services surged 1.3% to lead S&P 500 sectors, while consumer discretionary declined 0.7%, dragged down by Tesla, which fell 3%. The materials sector rose 0.3% amid gold prices topping $5,000 an ounce, boosting miners including Newmont Corp.

Shares of USA Rare Earth jumped 7.9% after reports that the U.S. government would acquire a 10% stake in the miner through a $1.6 billion debt-and-equity package. CoreWeave rose 5.7% following a $2 billion investment announcement from Nvidia.

Conversely, Intel fell 5.7% after forecasting quarterly results below expectations, and prison operators GEO Group and CoreCivic tumbled on political backlash over ICE funding.

Investors are also eyeing the Fed’s two-day meeting, where a hold on interest rates is widely expected, though market participants are looking for guidance on the central bank’s future rate path.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners on the NYSE and Nasdaq, with trading volumes slightly above the 20-day averages.

Reuters

data.title: Stock Picks: Eco World, 99 Speed Mart, UEM Sunrise And Hume Cement
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has highlighted several stocks with near-term trading potential following bullish technical breakouts, citing strong volume and improving price momentum.

Eco World Development Bhd is expected to extend its upward trajectory after breaking above the RM2.26 resistance on heightened volume. RHB Research noted the formation of a long bullish candlestick, signalling strong buying interest and accelerating momentum. The research house sees the stock heading towards RM2.37, followed by RM2.48. However, a drop below the RM2.15 support would invalidate the bullish setup and indicate a resumption of the prior correction.

99 Speed Mart Retail Bhd has also been flagged for further upside after pushing past the RM3.95 resistance. The counter climbed above this level with a fresh bullish candlestick, reflecting strong positive momentum. RHB Research expects the stock to advance towards RM4.15 and RM4.35 next. A fall below the RM3.75 support would negate the positive setup and trigger a correction phase.

Meanwhile, UEM Sunrise Bhd is regaining upward traction after holding above its 21-day simple moving average and staging a bullish breakout. The stock closed above the RM0.68 resistance, reinforcing a bullish structure. RHB Research said this sets the stage for a move towards RM0.73, with further upside potential to RM0.78. The bullish view would be negated if the stock falls below the RM0.63 support.

Hume Cement Industries Bhd is also poised for further gains after breaching the RM3.55 resistance on strong volume. RHB Research highlighted the emergence of a bullish candlestick, signalling a pick-up in upward momentum. Following the breakout, the stock is seen advancing towards RM3.75 and RM3.95. On the downside, a breach below the RM3.35 support would nullify the bullish setup and suggest a return to the prior corrective trend.

RHB Research said the highlighted counters continue to show constructive technical patterns, with volume confirmation supporting its short-term trading calls.

data.title: Bursa Opens Lower, ISF Leads Gains On Debut
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened lower on Wednesday, with the FBM KLCI down 5.82 points, or 0.33%, at 1,765.43 as of 9.15 am.

The broader market also saw declines, with the FBM70 and FBMEMAS falling 27.91 points (-0.16%) and 44.79 points (-0.35%) respectively.

Market activity was led by ISF Group Bhd, making its first day of trading on the ACE Market. The stock surged 17 sen, or 51.5%, to RM0.50, with 143.35 million shares changing hands.

Other early gainers included DLADY (+RM0.40 to RM33.42), HLFG (+RM0.24 to RM22.10) and Allianz (+RM0.16 to RM23.16).

On the losing side, Nestle (-RM1.60 to RM114.00), UTDPLT (-RM0.38 to RM30.40) and MPI (-RM0.32 to RM32.90) weighed on the market.

Trading volume at the start of the session was moderate, with investors keeping a watchful eye on heavyweight counters amid cautious sentiment.

data.title: Hang Seng Index Futures Power Past 27,000 As Bulls Tighten Grip, RHB
data.description:
Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) extended their bullish run after surging past the key 27,000-point psychological level, reinforcing positive momentum and validating a continuation of the prevailing uptrend, according to RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research).

The research house said HSIF climbed 321 points to settle at 27,069 points, after opening at 26,752 points. The index briefly dipped to an intraday low of 26,675 points before staging a strong rally to a session high of 27,194 points. It eventually closed near the day’s peak, forming a bullish candlestick that signals firm conviction among buyers.

Momentum carried into the evening session, with HSIF adding a further 117 points to trade around 27,186 points, underscoring sustained buying interest.

RHB Research noted that the successful breakout above the 27,000-point resistance marks a significant technical development. The index remains comfortably above both its 20-day and 50-day simple moving average lines, indicating a resumption of the broader uptrend. The relative strength index is also trending higher, suggesting that bullish momentum is gaining pace.

“We retain a positive trading bias and expect HSIF to extend its gains towards the next resistance levels,” RHB Research said, reiterating its call to maintain long positions.

The research house continues to recommend holding the long position initiated at 21,416 points, set on 14 April 2025. For risk management, the stop-loss level is maintained at 25,000 points.

On the technical front, immediate support is seen at 25,600 points, followed by a stronger floor at 25,000 points. On the upside, resistance is pegged at 27,400 points, with a subsequent target at the 28,000-point level.

RHB Research added that as long as HSIF stays above key support zones, the near-term outlook remains constructive, with bulls firmly in control of the trend.

data.title: HSIF Holds Above Key SMA Lines, RHB Advises Longs
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its positive trading bias on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) after the contract posted a fourth consecutive rebound on Monday.

HSIF added 30 points to close at 26,748 points, after opening at 26,709 points. The index touched an intraday low of 26,625 points and a high of 26,929 points, forming a bullish Doji candlestick by the close. During the evening session, HSIF extended gains, rising 135 points to last trade at 26,883 points.

RHB Research noted that the relative strength index has turned higher, signalling an improvement in bullish momentum and giving the technical advantage to the bulls. With price action holding above the rising 20-day and 50-day simple moving average lines, the broader market structure remains firmly in bullish territory.

If positive momentum persists, the research house said HSIF may retest the 27,400-point resistance. A decisive breakout above this level could propel the index towards the next resistance at 28,000 points.

RHB Research reiterated its recommendation for traders to retain long positions initiated at 21,416 points, set on April 14 2025. To manage downside risk, a stop-loss is fixed at 25,000 points.

The initial support levels are seen at 25,600 points and 25,000 points, while immediate resistance is pegged at 27,400 points, followed by 28,000 points.
